steps.title: Identify the input
steps.content: In the function $B(t)=70(2)^t$, the variable $t$ represents the time, in days, since the number of yeast cells was first recorded.

steps.title: Identify the output
steps.content: The value $B(t)$ represents the predicted number of yeast cells in the culture at that time. Therefore, $B(5)$ is the prediction at $5$ days after the first recording.

steps.title: Interpret the statement
steps.content: The statement $B(5)=2{,}240$ means that when $t=5$, the predicted number of yeast cells is $2{,}240$. In context, this says the predicted number after $5$ days was $2{,}240$. The answer is A.
